rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MOITOZA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 17, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Flowserve Corporation¿s motion for summary adjudication is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MOITOZA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 17, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Albay Construction Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence and taken off calendar as to false representation, strict liability and punitive damages. The declaration of Dave Roberts creates a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing insulation disturbed by defendant at a time when plaintiff was not employed by defendant. Plaintiff represents that he dismissed his claims for false representation, strict liability and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MOITOZA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 17, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary adjudication is taken off calendar as to issues 1 and 2 (strict liability and false representation) and granted as to issue 3 (punitive damages). Plaintiff represents that he dismissed his claims for strict liability and false representation. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present admissible evidence creating a triable issue that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. The evidence presented by plaintiff (the excerpts of the workers¿ compensation cases, the deposition testimony of defendant¿s personnel and the declaration of Dr. Cohen), considered collectively and liberally construed, do not show that defendant knew that the asbestos-containing insulation that it exposed plaintiff to created a safety hazard for plaintiff. Nor does this evidence show that defendant consciously disregarded information that it created a safety hazard for plaintiff. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MOITOZA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 17, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE HARTMAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 17, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant IMO Industries, Inc.¿ motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 and 2 (negligence and strict liability) and taken off calendar as to issues 3 and 4 (false representation and punitive damages). Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. Pages 376-386 of plaintiff¿s deposition transcript preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar with regard to plaintiff¿s exposure to asbestos-containing gaskets attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s separate statement does not include facts sufficient to establish that it is likely to prevail on its defense of sophisticated user/intermediary. There are no facts regarding either the plaintiff¿s or the Navy¿s knowledge or constructive knowledge of asbestos-containing gaskets. Plaintiff dismissed his claims for false representation and punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEVY SHIELDS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 17, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Rich- Tex, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1, 2 and 3 (negligence, strict liability and false representation) and granted as to issue 4 , which was erroneously identified in the notice of motion as issue 5 (punitive damages). The declaration of Mr. Hughes and defendant¿s responses to GO 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing taping compound, topping compound, general purpose compound and spray texture attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the declaration of Mr. Hughes are overruled. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any evidence creating a triable issue that defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEVY SHIELDS VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 17, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Alta Building Material Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product supplied by defendant. Defendant¿s objections to the deposition testimony of Mr. Hamilton, Hamilton¿s interrogatory responses and the declaration of Mr. Ay are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CRUZ HINOJOSA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder To Motion To Quash Service Of Plaintiffs Summons
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 17, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Johnson Controls, Inc.¿s joinder in defendant Rheem Manufacturing Co.¿s motion to quash service of summons is denied due to denial of Rheem¿s motion. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JUAN CRUZ HINOJOSA et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Plaintiffs Summons
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, February 17, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Rheem Manufacturing Co.¿s motion to quash service of summons is denied due to the motion being filed an insufficient number days in advance of the hearing date. It appears that the moving party failed to consider that two additional days need to be added for motions that are e-served absent a court order or stipulation. = (220/HEK)
